methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Krevera is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Edward Schneeweiss that has raised $3M+. The valuation universe consists of one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the basis for valuation. Using total capital raised of $3M as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, this method implies a post-money valuation of $8M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable; while eleven same-sector peers exist, none yielded three or more datapoints within the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier necessary for reliable median derivation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median point of $8M. This range reflects the 2.5x multiplier applied to the $3M raised baseline, with the bounds capturing inherent uncertainty in stage-progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include the absence of stage-matched peers for validation, the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for Krevera's specific segment, and limited visibility into unit economics or market traction beyond the capital raised figure. A peer-based approach becomes feasible only with additional comparable data or disclosure of Seed-stage robotics companies at comparable scale.
